purpose: Machine-readable task-selection queue for future WellFit Codex/AI agents. It converts existing governance, work-map, route/API/feature registers, and safety constraints into safe next-task candidates, required first reads, stop rules, checks, and expected PR outputs without creating a parallel product architecture.
jsonValidationChecks: jq empty project-register/routes.json project-register/apis.json project-register/features.json project-register/internal-sources.json project-register/product-readiness.json

taskCandidates.title: Refresh documentation-only baseline and quality-check notes
taskCandidates.purpose: Keep current-state, TODO, and architecture notes aligned after baseline check runs without changing runtime behavior.
expectedPrOutput: List documentation/register files updated.
State that no product logic changed.
Report baseline commands and failures honestly.
Name the next safe documentation or registry task.

taskCandidates.title: Synchronize project-register entries with Work Map and current state
taskCandidates.purpose: Update machine-readable registers so routes, APIs, features, product readiness, source maps, workflow rules, and feedback constraints point to existing leading files.
expectedPrOutput: List registry keys changed.
Identify source files used for each new or changed register entry.
Explain anti-duplication checks performed.
State any stale or uncertain entries as risks.

taskCandidates.title: Review Route/API Drift and Concept-to-Code Gap warnings
taskCandidates.purpose: Resolve safe, verified drift/gap warnings from the validation-only detectors without changing runtime product logic or protected high/critical areas.
expectedPrOutput: List each detector warning reviewed and whether it was resolved or deferred.
State that no product logic changed.
State protected high/critical areas were not auto-fixed.
Name the next safe registry-only task.

taskCandidates.title: Small existing UI route stability fix
taskCandidates.purpose: Make targeted fixes to existing pages while preserving the app shell, navigation, mobile patterns, and route register alignment.
expectedPrOutput: Name affected routes.
Describe UI behavior preserved or changed.
Include screenshot or explain why unavailable for perceptible UI changes.
State route/register alignment.

taskCandidates.title: API route planning or documentation alignment
taskCandidates.purpose: Plan or document API route changes before implementation, with security and authority boundaries explicit.
expectedPrOutput: Name proposed API authority level.
State security constraints and no-secret posture.
List affected features and registers.
Identify explicit approvals needed before runtime work.

taskCandidates.title: Feedback and analytics planning alignment
taskCandidates.purpose: Keep feedback, analytics, minimization, consent, and aggregate-agent-summary planning synchronized without live tracking changes.
expectedPrOutput: State data minimization boundaries.
Confirm no runtime tracking was added.
Name consent, retention, and aggregation assumptions.
List next approval needed for implementation.

taskCandidates.purpose: Inventory or plan AR/Unity work without touching PR #13 or Unity assets and without shifting reward authority to Unity.
expectedPrOutput: Confirm Unity files untouched.
Confirm PR #13 untouched.
State AR emits events only and app/backend retain authority.
List planning files updated.

taskCandidates.title: Compliance-sensitive planning only
taskCandidates.purpose: Document scope, risks, and required human decisions for compliance-sensitive areas without modifying runtime logic or legal/compliance messaging.
expectedPrOutput: Classify the area as critical risk.
State that only planning changed.
List explicit approvals required before any implementation.
Name protected files intentionally left untouched.

taskCandidates.title: Maintain follow-up detection and PR outcome memory-loop records
stopConditions: Would modify product logic or protected compliance/economy/runtime areas.
Would auto-approve high or critical follow-ups.
Would create duplicate TODO or architecture files instead of updating mapped files.
expectedPrOutput: Report-only follow-up detection.
Dry-run PR outcome recording validation.
Updated existing memory-loop registers and mapped TODO docs.

taskCandidates.title: Maintain the WellFit product readiness matrix
taskCandidates.purpose: Keep product readiness statuses, risk levels, blockers, safe next tasks, and advancement checks synchronized with existing registers and Work Map without changing product logic.
expectedPrOutput: List readiness modules changed.
Identify status/risk/check changes and source files used.
Confirm no product logic or Unity files changed.
State any human-review-required module decisions as risks.

taskCandidates.purpose: Keep WellFit visual/screenshot route validation mapped to the existing route register, Work Map, quality gate, and non-blocking browser-skip policy without changing product logic.
expectedPrOutput: List visual-regression docs/register/script files updated.
State browser checks are optional and non-blocking when unavailable.
Report route-register validation and browser skip/pass result honestly.
Confirm no product logic, protected compliance areas, or Unity files changed.
Name the next safe visual QA hardening task.

taskCandidates.title: Maintain imported Master Roadmap task registry
taskCandidates.purpose: Keep the imported WellFit Master Roadmap task registry synchronized with Work Map, TODO index, Product Readiness and internal sources without changing product logic or protected areas.
expectedPrOutput: List master-roadmap registry/doc/register files updated.
Confirm no runtime product logic, Unity files or protected compliance/economy/privacy areas changed.
Report master-roadmap-task-check and quality-gate results.
Name the next safe registry-only roadmap reconciliation task.

taskCandidates.title: Create internal-first research recommendation report
taskCandidates.purpose: Analyze an unclear WellFit product or technical question using internal sources first, optional external research only when explicitly available, three options, one recommendation, risk classification, and human-review-required decision status before any implementation.
expectedPrOutput: List internal sources reviewed before any external research.
State whether external research was available, used, or unavailable.
Summarize three options and exactly one recommended option.
Classify risk and mark humanReviewRequired for unclear, high, critical, or protected decisions.
Confirm no runtime product logic, Unity/PR #13 files, or protected compliance/economy/privacy areas changed.
Name the next safe review or registry-only task.

taskCandidates.title: Maintain planning-only Adaptive User Insight governance
taskCandidates.purpose: Keep aggregate Adaptive User Insight governance aligned with feedback, analytics, research-recommendation, product-readiness, and safety guardrails without activating live tracking or runtime product logic.
forbiddenFiles: functions/** runtime logic unless explicitly assigned
stopConditions: activationState would change away from planning_only
raw user data or user identifiers would become agent-visible
live tracking, runtime personalization, reward authority, health, child, location, camera/biometric, token, wallet, payment, betting, privacy, legal, or compliance logic would be modified
expectedOutput: Only aggregate signals above minimum thresholds are agent-visible.
High/critical and protected-topic recommendations remain human-review-required and implementationAllowed=false.
Research & Recommendation governance remains preserved and referenced, not duplicated.
expectedPrOutput: State that Adaptive User Insight remains planning_only and validation-only.
Confirm only aggregate signals are allowed and raw user identifiers/sensitive data are forbidden.
Confirm no runtime product logic, live tracking, Unity/PR #13 files, or protected compliance/economy/privacy areas changed.
List adaptive-user-insight-check, research-recommendation-check, JSON validation, and quality-gate results.
Name the next safe registry-only or human-review task.

taskCandidates.title: Maintain WellFit Agent Autopilot runbook and dry-run orchestrator
taskCandidates.purpose: Keep the single Autopilot entrypoint aligned with existing WellFit agent governance without changing runtime product logic or protected systems.
expectedPrOutput: List Autopilot documentation, register, and script files changed.
State that the dry-run orchestrator does not write files, merge, deploy, or implement product logic.
Report Autopilot dry-run output and standard checks.
Name the next safe governance or registry task.

taskCandidates.title: Maintain WellFit cross-reference maintenance framework
taskCandidates.purpose: Keep the cross-reference maintenance registry, docs, validator, workflows, task queue, Work Map, TODO index, and Quality Gate aligned so future agents update all relevant references after changes.
stopConditions: Protected compliance, privacy, reward authority, payment, token/NFT/wallet, health, child, location, or Unity files need changes without explicit human approval.
The task would create a duplicate Work Map, TODO index, route/API registry, readiness matrix, or agent orchestrator.
expectedPrOutput: List changed cross-reference, governance, TODO, and validation files.
Summarize the current agent-system analysis and how future agents must update cross-references.
Report checks run and any failures or warnings honestly.
Confirm runtime product code, protected areas, Unity/PR #13, and compliance-critical logic were untouched.

taskCandidates.title: Maintain WellFit full repository inventory and coverage audit
taskCandidates.purpose: Keep the repository inventory, audit doc, and report-only validation aligned so future agents can see mapped, protected, unmapped, stale, duplicate, route, and API coverage before editing files.
stopConditions: Runtime product code changes are required.
Protected compliance, privacy, reward authority, payment, token/NFT/wallet, health, child, location, or Unity files need changes without explicit human approval.
The task would create a duplicate Work Map, route/API register, product-readiness matrix, inventory architecture, or agent-governance system.
The task would auto-delete stale or duplicate historical TODO/status context.
expectedOutput: Machine-readable repository inventory stays valid JSON.
Inventory check reports mapped, protected, unmapped, stale/duplicate, route, and API coverage without rewriting files.
Quality gate includes the inventory check as warning/report-based triage.
PR confirms runtime product code, protected areas, Unity/PR #13, and compliance-critical logic were untouched.
expectedPrOutput: List changed repository-inventory, audit-doc, validation-script, quality-gate, workflow, task-queue, autopilot, Work Map, TODO Index, and Current State files.
Summarize inventory counts, protected coverage, unmapped warnings, and stale/duplicate warnings.
Report repository-inventory-check, JSON validation, quality-gate, and standard build/check results honestly.
Confirm runtime product code, protected compliance areas, Unity/PR #13, and product logic were untouched.

selectionRules: Choose the highest-priority candidate that matches the user request and does not violate stop conditions.
Read progress-log and agent-work-log memory before ranking candidates so the picker does not loop forever on a just-completed baseline task.
If the latest relevant log entry completed the top-ranked task, skip that task for one immediate selection pass unless no other safe non-blocked task exists.
If a task touches protected areas, downgrade action to planning-only or stop for explicit human approval.
If existing WORK_MAP or project-register entries already cover the area, continue those files instead of creating duplicates.
If runtime code is needed but the user requested documentation/registry only, stop and report the mismatch.
If checks cannot run because of environment limitations, document the exact command and reason in the PR and final report.

repeatSelectionPolicy.recentlyCompletedRule: Before selecting a task, inspect the most recent completed task IDs from progress-log entries and completed agent-work-log entries. Treat that recent window as selection memory for this pass, including the latest baseline/registry pair when both appear.
repeatSelectionPolicy.cooldownGuidance: Do not select a recently completed task again immediately when another non-blocked low/medium-risk candidate with a valid definition-of-done key is available. Prefer a different taskCategory when the recent window shows repeated governance work.
repeatSelectionPolicy.fallbackWhenNoOtherSafeTaskExists: If every otherwise safe candidate is recently completed, the picker may select the highest-ranked recently completed task and must explain that no alternative safe task exists.
repeatSelectionPolicy.completedTaskHandling: Progress-log entries may identify a task by taskId, id, goal, notes, title, nextRecommendedTask, or another text field containing the queue task ID. Agent-work-log entries use status done plus taskId. Logs are memory inputs only and do not mark queue candidates as permanently done.

loopGuardPolicy.rule: If AGENT-DOC-BASELINE-CHECK and AGENT-REGISTRY-SYNC both appear in the recent completed-task window, skip both once when another non-blocked low/medium-risk task with a valid definition-of-done key exists.
loopGuardPolicy.differentCategoryPreference: After the guarded pair, prefer the highest-ranked safe candidate whose taskCategory is not documentation_baseline or registry_sync. If no different-category candidate exists, select the safest available candidate and report the fallback reason.
